title: AI Product Readiness Assessment
description: Schema for validating AI product readiness assessment YAML files used in the AI PM Playbook. Each assessment evaluates a product across 11 dimensions, scoring readiness from 0 (not ready) to 5 (fully ready) with supporting evidence, risks, ownership, next actions, and a launch recommendation.

name.description: Name of the product being assessed.

owner.description: Team or role that owns this product.

target_users.description: List of target user types for this product.

use_case.description: Describes the use case the AI product addresses.

problem.description: Description of the problem being solved.

ai_job.description: What the AI component does to address the problem.

non_ai_alternative.description: How users solve this problem today without AI.

expected_outcome.description: What success looks like for this use case.

dimensions.description: Readiness scores across all 11 assessment dimensions.

problem_fit.description: How well the problem is suited for an AI solution.

workflow_fit.description: How well the AI integrates into existing user workflows.

ai_job_definition.description: Clarity and specificity of what the AI is expected to do.

data_readiness.description: Availability and quality of data needed to build and run the AI.

eval_readiness.description: Ability to measure whether the AI is performing well.

system_behavior.description: Defined behavior for edge cases, errors, and uncertainty.

risk_and_safety.description: Identification and mitigation of risks including bias, harm, misuse, and agentic risks (tool misuse, goal hijacking, error cascading).

regulatory_readiness.description: Risk classification, data provenance, transparency requirements, and applicable legal, privacy, or sector-specific requirements.

cost_and_business_case.description: Economic viability and ROI justification for the AI product.

observability.description: Ability to monitor, debug, and understand AI behavior in production.

launch_and_operations.description: Readiness for deployment, rollback, and ongoing operations.

recommendation.description: Recommended next release stage based on the weighted score, individual dimension scores, and hard blockers.

weighted_score.description: Weighted readiness score from 0.0 to 5.0, calculated from dimension scores and weights.

conditions.description: Conditions attached to the recommended next stage.

blockers_before_pilot.description: Issues that must close before a pilot can begin.

blockers_before_production.description: Issues that must close before customer-facing production launch.

blockers_before_scale.description: Issues that must close before broad rollout or scale-up.

alternative_recommendation.description: A non-AI or lower-risk alternative the team should consider.

dimension.description: A single readiness dimension with a score, evidence, risks, ownership, and next action.

score.description: Readiness score from 0 (not ready) to 5 (fully ready).

evidence.description: Supporting evidence for the assigned score.

risks.description: Known risks or concerns for this dimension.

owner.description: Person or role responsible for this dimension.

next_action.description: Concrete next step to improve readiness in this dimension.
